summary: Crystal‑clear soundboard captures a solid early‑90s show with standout jams on “Stranger”, “Let It Grow” and an unforgettable “All All Over Now, Baby Blue” encore.
review: The recording is praised as one of the cleanest SBD’s ever released – instruments are distinct, the mix round and full. The performance is a strong first set and a second set that, while occasionally clunky, delivers high‑energy moments such as “Stranger”, a savage 12‑minute “Let It Grow” and an all‑time great “All All Over Now, Baby Blue”. Overall fans find it a solid 1993 concert, though some note the “Way to Go” as a low point.

ai_rating.rationale: Recording quality is rated excellent; show quality is solid but not universally hailed as top‑tier for the year, yielding an overall rating around four stars.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Described as the cleanest SBD around – crisp, distinct instruments, round and full mix with no reported drop‑outs or muddiness.

standout_songs: All All Over Now, Baby Blue
show_quality.setlist_flow: First set is consistently strong; second set delivers big jams but feels a bit clunky in places, with uneven pacing despite several high points.

band_member_comments.Jerry: Repeatedly praised for his fat funky tone, soaring solos, and ‘Jimi‑lessons’ on Watchtower; also noted for vocal delivery on Peggy-O.
band_member_comments.Phil: Mentioned delivering “supremely phunky fills” during the Corrina jam.
band_member_comments.Bob: Highlighted for trading blues licks with Jerry on The Same Thing and adding extra verses to Corrina.
band_member_comments.Keys: Vince (keys) is lauded for co‑leading jams on Stranger, Let It Grow and other high‑energy moments.
band_member_comments.Drums: The drummers are noted for pushing the jam forward, especially during Queen Jane and the overall rhythmic drive.
